sentences.text: Hey, Derek.
Hey, guys.
Hey, Austin.
How you doing?
I'm doing well.
I'm out of the office later this… later this week, so I'm trying to scramble and get as many things in motion as possible.
Nice.
Are you going anywhere fun, or is it….
I am.
We are, like, Carlisle closes their offices globally, for a week in August.
They've been doing it since 2021, I think, or maybe even 2020.
It's COVID era, kind of thing.
Okay.
I'm gonna be going to Switzerland this week, so….
Nice.
I'm super jealous of that.
Have you been out there before, or, like, how'd you pick that?
No, never been.
Me and my wife, we kind of just pick our travel based on where we can find a decent direct flight.
Yeah.
So, we found some flights out of Dulles Airport in DC.
We're going for, like, the… Kind of like the Alpine Mountain Wellness theme this year.
Yeah.
So, we'll see.
I'm picturing, like, you know, broad mountain vistas, a hot tub on the mountainside.
That's my dream, so….
Yeah, that's, … do you get in… that's gonna be a little bit earlier in the year, like, so it's probably not quite snow season yet there, is it?
Or….
No, I don't think so.
I think it'll be pretty similar to, to the weather we're having here.
Okay.
Maybe a little bit cooler, just because of the altitude.
Right.
Yeah, I'm expecting it to be, you know, 60s, 70s, somewhere around there.
Yeah, that'll be perfect.
I'm a… I used to live out west, so big into, like, out skiing and the mountains, so the Alps has always been on my list to get out there.
Yeah, yeah, I mean, I love getting out west to, like, to Colorado and to Tahoe, and I usually only get, like, one trip a year.
Don't… I don't want to make it sound like I'm a mountain bunny, but, like.
Yeah.
It's always a good time.
No, that's super jealous, and I'm glad you guys get a full week.
We actually… so I brought on our senior product manager, Mitch, here, but we just got back all last week.
We were at a company retreat in Chicago, so….
Oh, nice, cool.
Yeah.
Yeah, I mean, everybody does their own little thing.
It's nice to just take a break, one way or another.
We, … We did, I mean, it's nice that the company does it, right?
Like, because we… there's a lot of different groups.
I'm in the real estate group, but … since it's, like, firm-wide, right, it's easier for us to kind of turn it off.
Right?

Instead of just taking a week vacation where you're still getting pinged, and … I mean, regardless of what we say, we're still gonna be getting emails from our partners and managers and everything, but this is, … it's something to plan around every year, at the very least.
Yeah, that's awesome, and … maybe we should coach for that, Michigan, at least a full, full.
Why not?
I was about to say, I wouldn't mind if we did something similar.
It's nice, it's a good setup.
I think they learned that people were just… again, this was COVID era, but … I think, like, some New York banks do it, so… I'm into it.
From somebody who rarely takes more than, like, a Friday or, like, a Monday off, you know?
Right.
off here and there, but I'm not taking bigger vacations.
This, … this is good reason to do so.
For sure, yeah, it's guilt-free.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
That's a good way of looking at it.
Yeah.
Well, I don't want to take too much of your time then.

I appreciate you jumping on to… just kind of wanted to get, your feedback so far on the trial, and then I also mentioned, you had some specific requests that pitch in on, kind of on, like, the high-level portfolio reporting.
So, Mitch, I'm glad you're on.
Because I did… I explored it quite a bit, and it's not like I'm unfamiliar with what the capabilities are, because a lot of our managers already use ApartmentIQ or your competitor, right?

And I get… you know, I was telling Austin this, but, you know, I have… I don't know, 30 active deals right now, and then, you know, a bunch that are still in development that aren't operating yet, which means I get… not all of them, but the large majority of them I get daily emails on, like, daily activity emails, and then weekly we get a market survey, a market survey email, and a daily, weekly summary.
And the surveys all, you know, take the same form.

It's comp set analysis, and that's what your guys' product currently, at least from my exploration, like, what it's really… You know, what it accomplishes is, … you know, it's a really tight package to summarize the comp set, where average rents are, where average occupancy is, weekly leasing totals, that kind of thing.
And I have… I want to keep that in the hands of our, … sorry, my throat's already going because I'm… I started the week pretty aggressively on calls, but … My intention is not to… duplicate work.
Like, I just don't have enough time to do that.
Like, I don't… I'm not going to be using these reports the same way our property manager is using these reports, because that's their job.
Our property manager, our regional manager, I even have a partner that should be overseeing all of that.
So, if I'm doing it, then there's… and there's reason for me to do it.
Like, if the deal's going sideways, or we're not seeing leasing activity.
And I don't exactly… Trust what the property team is telling me, or what the manager's telling me, or what our partner's telling us.
I'll roll up my sleeves and dig in and try to understand what's going on.
So, if I'm getting to that level where I'm actually utilizing these reports, I'm just gonna call them, like, the… The comp reports, the property management comp reports.
then there's probably an issue with the team.
The bigger picture I see, and that's what I'd like to talk to you about more, is, …
what I'm trying to do… with this, and with a bunch of other toolkits, is… Streamline our management.
like, Carlisle's management.
I work with probably… Our real estate group, I think, is 150 people, in DC.
We have probably…
70 asset managers from different levels, like, you know, maybe 40 of those are associates up to me, or, you know, the next level, MD level.
And we're each on a ton of deals.
We're each on, like, 20, 25 deals, so it's not a matter of, can we get the information?
We have ways of getting the information, it's how can we…
efficiently How can we aggregate and summarize the information into a useful digest?
And how… what metrics are important to us to direct our… our weekly… to-do list, if you will.
Sure.
So what I imagine is, you know, what's important to us, this is just, like, one… One case, one use of the information.
I would want to know something like.
get… and this is just me describing what would be helpful for me, but, like, a weekly email that's sent out to me that summarizes all of my deals, right?
All 25 of them, and it just picks up key metrics, like.
week over week.
concession change in concession in the comp set.
Week over week, change in market rent in the comp set, right?
Like, everything else that goes in the comp report, average rents, what this specific comp did, what this specific comp did.
I have a way of getting that information.
I could click through, and getting 30 of those emails hitting my inbox all on a Monday, it just becomes overwhelming.
So, what I want to boil it down to is, like, one email that tells me which ones I should focus on a weekly basis, right?
Like, there's gonna be movers, and there's going to be ones that stay stagnant.
And what that helps me do is… have the conversations that drive value at the end of the day, like, okay, if… This comp set is seeing a reduction in concessions week over week.
that gives me a tip, like, that tells me I can… I should go to this team, confirm what the report's telling me, right?
Like, boots on the ground, are you guys seeing this?
Because the data's saying it.
And then I, you know, I could do my own research, click through, see which comps exactly… reduced it.
And this is not something I would do on every single one, right?
Like, I just need to start at a high level and figure out what I need to focus on.
And then from there, I can… I can direct teams, but… I think what I've learned is I can't… synthesize the information myself.

I can't, you know, on a Monday, go through 30 deals worth of comp reports, and then… if I did, right, then I would know what I need to follow up on, what I need… what direction I need to give my teams, what needs to get done in a week, where we should be pushing rents.
But it just becomes an entire day worth of, like.
Looking at information.
Yeah.
So… What are your thoughts?
Like, is that… do you think that's possible?

One thing is, like, obviously you guys have really worked on putting together a nice you know, PDF report that is catered probably towards the property manager putting together a very nice, professional-looking report, right?
And then hitting send to whoever… their regional, their partners, whoever that gets it.
It's a nice report.
But it… for me, like, It'd almost be better if it was, like, the… data that I was interested in could be delivered in, like, an Excel form.
Right?
And then from there, I can manipulate, drop it into a template that Carlisle uses.
And then kind of figure it out from there, right?
Like, so getting that data, that's, like, part two of what I'm asking for right now.
Getting that data in an Excel form would be very useful on our end, too.
Okay.
Yeah, Derek, I think everything you shared, thank you for that.
I mean, super helpful context.
A little bit of background, this is our first time on a call together, so….
I just gave you a lot, like, in 3, 4, I might have been going off for, like, 8 minutes right there, but….
No, no, that was awesome.
Yeah, my role, I have a fun role.
My job is talking to customers, prospects, like, any potential users of ApartmentIQ, and figuring out ways how we can make the product better and more useful for you.
So this is exactly what I love to hear.
The timing couldn't be better, because we're actually kicking off… we have two initiatives in flight that I think will help with what you're describing.
One of them is we're working on an overall portfolio report.
And the use case there is it's for people like you, like, it's for people who want a higher level summary of everything.
They don't necessarily need to get down into what a specific comp is doing in a specific you know, like, comp set, and getting down into the nitty-gritty of every single one.
So we're actually pretty….
pause right there, because I think… In one way, it's like…

I guess the result, right, for it to be useful, it does have to be higher level, just what you described, like, we need to know what are the actionable, … what's actionable on a weekly basis, and we get that from those higher level stats, like, this is what the change is in this comp set.
But… to get there, it's almost like we need more granular data, right?
Like, I'm starting to work with some bigger data sets, and they're all taking the form of, whatever, long-form Excel files that are, like.
here's the, you know, here's a time series going vertically, and it's, like, effective rents for each week.
And I don't know how granular your data can get, right?
Like, I played with some of the reports, and I could see historical 30-day, 60-day, 90 day, whatever, 3, 6, 9 months, or whatever.

So, it's almost like getting access, and I don't know if you guys would ever provide… maybe you will, but like… getting access to the granular week-over-week data for each of those costs, because to your point, I do kind of care what individual comps are doing, it's just how it's going to be presented in final form is going to be a summary of all of that movement, right?

And whether, on your guy's end, with your software, it's performing those calculations to determine what that summary changes, versus us getting all that information for all the deals, and then summarizing it ourself.
The end… the goal end result is still, like, a nice digest that gives us something actionable each week.
Yeah, gotcha.
So a couple of things on that, … The… as far as, like, how granular our data gets, we do have unit-level data that's updated daily.
And our historical data will go back.
in that way, too.
So if you… if you wanted to, theoretically, you can go back over 5 years.
I think, Austin, correct me if I'm wrong, I think 5 years is… the furthest back it goes, and that'll all be daily unit level for every single property that goes back that far.
And then, so a few things, too.
I know it sounds like most of the reports that you're seeing from us are in PDFs that are delivered to you in your email.
Hmm.
Have you logged into ApartmentIQ at all, like, into our actual web interface?
Yeah, I'm in it right now, actually.
Okay.
Yeah.

Yeah, because there's a number of different, like, you know, we've got our market survey where you can easily benchmark your subject against comps, and then there's a number of other reports that you could kind of toggle through that all just aggregate the data in different ways and kind of give you visuals.
So… and most of them have a timeframe filter at the top that you can play around with, so if you're looking at a 7-day versus a 30-day versus a 2-year comparison.
You should be able to toggle to those.
Derek, I did also want to just ask you.
Are you only receiving our PDF reports?
Because we do have Excel as an option.
I am getting some Excel reports.
Okay.
Yeah.
But I think it, …
No, I am getting some Excel reports that I play around with.
I'm not sure… and that's from, like, partners like Graystar, or not Graystar, because they use the other one, but from other partnerships, they send them around, and I've poked around in them a bit.
Okay, cool.

Yeah, so those are available to you and your entire team, too, so if you prefer that format, you should be able to start using them, or have your team start sending those instead of the PDFs, if you want something a little more interactive that you could actually configure on your own, rather than the PDF.

But then the other thing, as far as reporting at the overall portfolio level goes, we're going to be building an entire new dashboard in the application, and then the idea there is that once we have the view in the application, you'll be able to create exports around that, too, and we'll get better at delivering multiple exports to you.
One of the pain points we've heard from customers is it's kind of annoying to get, like.
10, 15, 20 emails every Monday morning, so simple things like putting it all in one email instead and, like, putting it in a zip file.
So, we're working on those types of improvements.
If you'd be up for it, I would love to share my screen and just get your honest feedback on What do you think?
No, for sure.
I'm looking at one right now, so you can go first, and then we can, maybe.
Sure.
Walkthrough process, yeah.
Okay, cool.
So what I'm about to show you, this is just a design mock-up.
The numbers are all fake, so the math won't math, but the idea here is that if you looked at your portfolio summary, you could see a table of all of your subject properties.
Along with key metrics.
so that you could quickly analyze the performance of each, and then the idea here is you would be able to select any one of these, and it would lead you into the market survey.
So I'll kind of pause there.
I can provide more details on anything, but … would love to just, you know, take a moment, see what resonates, see what we could do better.
No, this is, … This is kind of what I was thinking of, right?
And the lift would be… so to set this up, you would have all your different subjects, or all the deals in my portfolio.
What I'd be interested in would be the comp set, right?
So we would hand-select the you know, 3 to 10 deals, right?
That would fit under each one, and this would be a summary of that, right?
Yep, exactly.
Available units, rent, we'll just say that's, like, you know, effective rent or something.
The number of leasing.
Leases and apps, which… … What's the difference between apps and leases?
So, you know.
One's submitted applications, one's actual leases?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
For us, since we're public data only, we infer the application.
So essentially, we just put any unit that comes down from online leasing, we give it a 7-day wait period.
And, if it stays offline, we assume it's a successful lease.
If it's relisted, then we'll consider that, like, a canceled, lease or, denial, and it'll appear as a relisted unit.
So, if I wanted to punch through… well, one, I guess this covers… this is the point in time, this is Monday morning report, this tells me how many available units, what the average rent is.
What it doesn't tell you is what actually moved, right?
Like, so if I look at subject prop, property A, right, like, the rent, $2,800,
I guess the comparison, right, like.
the subject versus the comps.
We're at 2800, the comp set's at 1900.
That number, in itself, It's useful for establishing In my role, like, where we should be relative to the comps.

But the more important thing, at least, you know, in my job, and again, giving direction, what's actionable at the end of the day, the more important thing for me is, is that 4% differential for that specific cell right there?
Is that going up or down week over week, or 30 days over 30 days?
Are we still maintaining that 4%?
well, it looks like it's much more than… I know nothing's tying out, so maybe… but that, … in that example would be, like, a $900 differential, right?
Maybe we have a really… it's a high-rise versus a bunch of little garden-style deals, right?
Now… Right, and… say, next week, we're at $2,800 still, and the comp set average is $2,100, right?
I would have to remember that last week we were at a $900 premium, and this week, now, we've eroded a ton of value relative to the comp set, and we're at $700.
Right?
So, it's almost like… that's why the… The time series is really important to us, because it allows us to track, like.
where we should… we call it… I mean, maybe you guys call it the same thing.
We call it just, like, penetration.
Should our penetration be 110% of the comp set?
Should it be 90%?
So, in the beginning, we established it, we're at 110%.
over a year, are we still at 110%, or are we at 120 or 100, right?
Like, and that's how we try to manage our teams.
Our comp set raised rents by 10 bucks, we want to raise rents by 10 bucks to maintain our same level.
Does that make sense?
It does, absolutely.
So, in this instance, it's like, these values are useful, but really what you want to see is how much each of these has moved up or down since the previous time you've looked at.
Yeah.
And, I mean, one way of doing that would be….

I mean, the really tricky way of doing it would be, like, okay, establishing what that penetration should be, you know, 110% of the comp set or something like that, and then tracking relative to that where we are.

That becomes a little trickier, just because, you know, maybe a new deal opens, and that we were at 110% in the beginning, but then a really high-end deal opens, and it's like… You know, it throws everything off, and all of a sudden, as an average, we're at 105, and we should be at 105.
So, another way of doing it is kind of what I described, and it's like, well, the comp set, they're at 1932 this week.
Next week, if I had a report that said the comp set just increased to… $2,000 on average?
Right?
Like, I would need something that showed comp set.
I miss as much detail as possible, but like… Comset increase 5% from $1,900 to $2,000.
I know, because I'm in charge of my deals, I didn't approve any increases.
I wish if the team asked me to increase, right?
Like… I have a good handle of what we're doing, but seeing that the comp set increased by $100, should we be increasing by $100, right?
It's giving me the data on the comp set that I'm not tracking as closely to kind of lead what my strategy is for that week.
Sure, that makes sense.
So it's mostly, like, tell me what's changed, rather than….
That's changed.
Yeah, rather than just benchmark me against my comps, because it sounds like you're fairly familiar with that on any given point in time, unless something has changed.
Yep, the snap, yeah, exactly.
So… Yeah, it's… it's what's changed.
That's the big conversation each week.
So, I mean, and that leads so many of our decisions on a weekly basis.
I mean, that's how I do my job.
It's like…
we have a blowout week.
We have 15 net leases, right?
We feel like we're doing great.
Next week, we haven't changed a thing, and we have zero net leases, right?
First thing I'm trying to do is figure out what in the comp set changed.
And this report, if I looked at it, I would say, I can't really tell, right?
Like, I can see that we're at a 4%, you know, 4% premium to the comp set.
What were we before when we hit 15 net leases?
Were we… Were we… was this… you know, did the comp set drop rents, and now we're, like, way overpriced?
I just can't tell.
Sure.
How important is it, then, like, just understanding a little bit of what you're after here?
Is it important for you to see this actual value?
as, like, a gut check from time to time, right?
Like, you don't wanna…
I don't want to let that get away from me, right?

Because we go into these deals, and that's what's gonna line up against our budget, or when I do, like, a next, like a… you know, a sale pro forma, and I start talking to brokers, I want to make sure that we're leasing at the right rate.
But again, on, like.
Most of my work week is filled with leasing calls, and strategy calls and, like.
what action are we taking on a weekly basis?
Re-establishing the fact that we're at $1,800, I mean… we're not, sorry, $2,800 in this example.
it's something I should already know, right?
Like, I should already know that.
Yeah, so in that instance, it seems like, really, the core insight that you'd be after is this percent difference, and then the change in time that you were mentioning.
Yeah, like, I would want to know if the concession… if the comp set dropped concessions by 5%, right?
Like, last week, the effective concession was 10%, and now it's down to 6%, right?
And then we could click through and see exactly, week over week, who changed.
Or, it'd be great if we can do that on a… on… an adjustable time period.
We're just talking week over week, because that's the cadence of my reports, but if I had a report that said week-over-week change.
trailing 30-day change, trailing 90-day change, that would be my ideal, right?
Because then I could say, this is the trend in the comp set, the comp set… Over the last 90 days, we see this downward trend on concessions.
Over the last 7 days, it's continued to go down.
And then I compare that to what my property's been doing, and it's like, we haven't… we haven't dropped concessions, what's wrong?
Right?
Like….
So, okay, that's super helpful.
One of the ideas here was to add exactly what you were saying, a timeframe filter, so that's what you're seeing at the top here.

And then that way, you can configure it, so whether you're having, like, weekly pricing calls, or if you're doing a strategy call, that's maybe less frequent, every 30 days, once a quarter with executives, or anything like that.
No, it'd be cool, because I assume when you say, with that, that filter that lasts 30 days, if you change that, and this is a mock-up, right?
Is that, like, average available units, the average rent, the average exposure over that time period?
What would you like to see there?
It would almost be like, if I could have this table that you have, this is… I mean, maybe we're getting there right now.
This exact table, exposure, available units, rent.

I don't know if leases would apply or apps would apply, but even if it's just rent, say rents and effective rents, two columns where in I could define what time period I'm looking at that, like, last 30 days.
well, I guess it wouldn't be, we wouldn't call it last 30 days, just say, like.
average in August versus average in… have, like, two different time periods where I could define the two, and then a third column that shows the variance between the two.
Okay.
Yes, I would prefer to see, rather than, like, one single.
average for the time period, you'd prefer to see a point in time start of period to end period.
Yeah.
It would almost be like, okay, I'd like to see… I mean, I'll just do it simply, but I… in the back end, if it would be like… I'll just do it, I mean, I'll just say it simply, like.
average rents in August versus average rents in May, right?

I'll just pick up anything, and then you could adjust the August rents would… would basically represent what our current comp set is, like, current performance, current comp set, current… Subject property rents.

And then you'd just be able to define what that prior period comparison was Because the insight, the key insight is, like, what's… again, what's changed if I see… if I make that comparison and say, again, it's, like, August average rents compared to May average rents.
And then I see… you know, that variant, I'm just gonna… Think of that variance column now.
I see that over the last 3 months, the subject property, which is my property, hasn't changed market rents, we haven't changed our concession offering.
Our occupancy's ticked up because we've continued to lease up, whatever.
And then I compare it to the comp set, and it's like, comp set has pushed rents 5%, Comp set has reduced concessions 10%.
I'd be like, there's something that's off here.
I don't think the team is performing, right?
Like, and then it's a conversation with my operating team to be like.
what's the issue here?
You know, if the comp set's really gotten this much more competitive, we would have… expected an uptick in leasing, right?
Because all of a sudden, we're… our value is so much higher, because there's no more concessions in the comp set, but we haven't… we haven't even touched our concessions.
Right, yeah.
That makes a ton of sense.
So you mentioned concessions a few times, obviously rent is important, available units sounds to be important… seems to be important.
The idea here with this hide show metrics is that you'd be able to configure which columns you see here.

Are there any that we haven't mentioned yet, or that you don't see on the screen that would be useful for you as you're kind of, like, triaging to understand which properties you need to dig into a little bit further?
Yeah, so the big, big picture is… we have a big portfolio, and I don't know if you've talked to any partners on this, but, like, we're trying to identify when we can sell deals.
Like, that's our business.
We, Carlisle, we… we develop with a bunch of development partners.
Sometimes we buy.
And then we wanna, …

sell when we think we're gonna… when we get, credit for the value that we've created, whether it's, you know, development and… and, and lease up and sell, what have you, but in this environment, it's gotten incredibly difficult.
Capital markets are a little bit more punishing.
And what it comes down to.
You know, if you had asked me a couple years ago, all we would need to do is finish the construction.

lease up the property to stabilize it, or close to… sometimes you even need to lease up the property, sometimes you just need it to establish where your market rents are, and then you could sell the property at, like, 50% because the market was so high.
Yeah.
Today.
buyers are punishing.
You know, the capital markets are punishing, right?
Like, you have to finish the lease up, you have to have a few months of stabilized financials, you need to demonstrate the concessions are burning down, all that stuff.

So… the tool that I'm describing to you would help us Identify, one, operating issues, like, where there might be issues with our operating team or management team, where we might need to look at our people or our special, where we might need to make adjustments.
But then also… Which sub-markets, which comp sets.
are stabilizing, right?
Like, because there's been so much supply that's delivered that

comp sets, you know, if you look at the average, not just, like, the stabilized property occupancy, but, like, average occupancies, they're low right now, you know, because there's so many deals in lease up.
So the only other one I would add would be, you know, market rent, as you mentioned, concession level, and then occupancy.
And I would want in that occupancy number to, you know, include all the other deals and lease up, right?
And … So that, you know, when I think of… the strategy around sales, those are really the ones that I'm… that I need to check the box on, right?
Do our rents match The pro forma, like, sale target rents to get to a sale.
have our concessions started to burn down to a level that, you know, allows me to do that?
And then, is the sub-market stabilize, right?
Like, it's really difficult for us to sell A deal if there's 5 other deals and lease up.
I don't care how good.
Tons of new supply on the way, too, right?
Yeah, so what we're trying to track is, like, when is our little comp set?
You know, it's the comp set that we define, it's the radius that we define around a deal.
When are those different ways of looking at the comp set?
stabilized.
… And again, like, that time over time, like, that point in time compared to another point in time is helpful, because I could look at today and say.
Okay, the comp set is… you know.
Today, the comp set is 87% occupied, with an effective concession of 9%.
I could look back.
at 3 months ago, back in May, and see that the comp set was, on average, 78% occupied, with a 12% concession, right?
So, just with those two data points, I'd be able to say.
you know, concessions have come down some amount, and quantify how much concessions have come down over the 3-month period.
And then also, occupancy, we're nearing stabilization.
I could extrapolate a bit with the data that you give me.
I could say.
